title: Post-022 Operations Visibility Live-Data Boundary Decision
objective: Decide the smallest truthful next Operations Visibility slice after Packet 022 now that render-level browser comparison has passed and the live-data boundary has been traced to the controlling source files.
decision_rationale: Packet 021 proved that the public-host static surfaces render correctly in a real non-host browser, so the next unresolved boundary is live-data reachability rather than shell render viability.

The controlling code path is direct and local to the public page assets: `drivers.js`, `schedule.js`, `tracer.js`, `variance.js`, `approval-surface.html`, and `lead-ops/index.html` hardcode `http://localhost:8000/...` rather than resolving a deploy-aware public API base.
This means the next truthful move is not more browser comparison. It is a bounded source-local planning slice for public-host API-origin remediation on those static surfaces.

`integration-dashboard/index.html` already uses an origin-aware branch (`window.location.hostname === 'localhost' ? 'http://localhost:8000/api/v1' : window.location.origin + '/api/v1'`), which provides a nearby implementation reference without requiring a broad architecture search.

decision_evidence.non_goal: This packet does not yet implement the remediation or claim live-data success.

next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 024 - Bounded Public-Host API Origin Remediation Planning
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-05 as a decision-only packet. Packet 023 closes the post-022 boundary question by determining that the next truthful slice is a bounded planning or remediation lane for hardcoded public-host API bases inside the static Operations Visibility surfaces rather than more render-only browser comparison.
next_action: Author Packet 024 as the bounded public-host API-origin remediation planning slice using the direct public-page asset files as the controlling implementation surface.
